intro: Privacy Policy
Last Updated: 25.02.2026

Gragabo (“Gragabo”, “we”, “us”, “our”) respects privacy and is committed to protecting personal data and confidential business information entrusted to us by individuals and organisations that interact with our website, submit diagnostic information, or engage with our services.

This Privacy Policy describes, in a transparent and legally grounded manner, how we collect, use, disclose, transfer, store, and safeguard information, and the rights and choices available to data subjects.

No method of transmission or storage is completely secure. However, we maintain and continuously improve appropriate technical and organisational measures to protect information against accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorised disclosure, or access.

By accessing or using our website and services, you acknowledge that you have read and understood this Privacy Policy. Where consent is the legal basis for a specific activity, we will request it in a clear manner and you may withdraw it at any time.
Our accountability and internal consequences

We treat compliance as a governance obligation. We apply internal controls, confidentiality duties, and access restrictions. Any unauthorised access, misuse, or disclosure by personnel or contractors may result in corrective action, including access revocation, contractual remedies, and disciplinary measures (up to termination), and - where required - notification to competent authorities and affected individuals.
Legal scope and applicability
Serbian data protection law
Gragabo is a legal entity established in the Republic of Serbia. Our processing of personal data is conducted in full compliance with the applicable domestic legal framework, including:

* The Law on Personal Data Protection (“Zakon o zaštiti podataka o ličnosti”), published in the Official Gazette of the Republic of Serbia No. 87/2018, which regulates the collection, processing, storage, transfer, and protection of personal data, the rights of data subjects, the responsibilities of data controllers and processors, and the conditions for lawful data processing within Serbia. This law became effective on 21 August 2019 and mirrors many of the principles of the EU’s General Data Protection Regulation.

The Law applies to the processing of personal data conducted in whole or in part by automated means, as well as to non-automated processing that forms part of a data set intended to be processed, and extends to controllers and processors with establishment in Serbia, as well as to processing activities relating to data subjects residing in Serbia by entities without a Serbian establishment, where the processing activities are connected to offering goods or services or are otherwise tied to the territory.

The competent national authority for enforcement of the Law on Personal Data Protection is the Commissioner for Information of Public Importance and Personal Data Protection, which supervises compliance, handles complaints, and may impose administrative measures in accordance with the statutory framework.
GDPR territorial scope

In addition, the European Union’s General Data Protection Regulation (“GDPR”) (Regulation (EU) 2016/679) applies to us in respect of individuals who are physically present in the European Union or the European Economic Area (EEA) when we process their personal data in the context of offering goods or services or monitoring behaviour, regardless of where Gragabo itself is established. This extraterritorial application is set out in Article 3 of the GDPR and applies where the relevant criteria are met.
More specifically:

* Even though Gragabo is not established in the EU/EEA, the GDPR nonetheless applies to our processing activities when personal data of individuals located in the EU/EEA is processed in connection with the offering of goods or services or behavioural monitoring.

* This territorial scope applies regardless of the nationality or residence status of the individual data subject; the relevant criterion is the individual’s physical presence in the EU/EEA at the time of data collection or processing.
Global operations

Gragabo is aware that personal data protection regimes vary by jurisdiction. We therefore recognise that activities involving data subjects from other regions - such as CEFTA member states, the United States, Mexico, Russia, India, Asia, Africa, North America, South America, Europe, and Australia - may be subject to additional or alternative data protection and privacy laws applicable in those regions. Where such laws impose obligations that extend beyond this Policy, we will endeavour to comply with those legal requirements insofar as they lawfully apply to our operations.

title: 1. Information We Collect
intro: We collect and process information that identifies you (“Personal Data”) and information relating to your organisation (“Business Information”). We only collect the minimum amount of data necessary for the purposes described in this Policy, and we process it on lawful grounds as required by applicable data protection laws, including the Serbian Law on Personal Data Protection (Official Gazette of RS No. 87/2018) and, where applicable, the GDPR.

subsections.title: 1.1 Information Provided Directly by You
content: We may collect Personal Data and Business Information that you voluntarily provide when you:
* complete diagnostic forms, questionnaires, or assessments;
* submit contact or consultation requests;
* correspond with us by email, telephone, web chat, or other communication channels; or
* otherwise interact with us in the course of engaging our services.
Such information may include:
* full name;
* company or organisation name;
* job title or professional role;
* business address;
* email address;
* telephone number;
* business information relevant to your inquiry or engagement;
* strategic, financial, commercial, and operational data that you choose to share; and
* any other information you voluntarily supply to us in connection with your use of our website or services.
Examples may include uploaded documents, project briefs, business strategy descriptions, financial metrics, and operational details.
This category does not include information that has been made anonymous such that you can no longer be identified (“Anonymous Data”).

Lawful Basis for Processing: This information is processed based on your consent, on the basis of contractual necessity where services are provided to you, and/or on the basis of our legitimate interests in responding to your requests, providing services, and managing our relationship with you, subject always to applicable law.

subsections.title: 1.2 Automatically Collected Information
content: When you access or interact with our website and related online services, certain information is collected automatically through cookies, web beacons, and other tracking technologies. This information helps us understand how our website is used, improve user experience, and support analytical and security measures.
The categories of automatically collected information may include:
* IP address or similar network identifier (temporary or anonymised, as required by law);
* browser type and version;
* device type and system software (e.g., operating system);
* unique device identifiers (where technically necessary and lawful);
* pages visited and actions taken on our website;
* date and time stamps of visits;
* time spent on particular pages;
* referring website or source through which you arrived on our site; and
* standard click-stream data.

Automatically collected information does not ordinarily include information that directly identifies you unless you have previously provided such information in the same session or expressly logged in.
This data is collected through technologies such as:
* cookies (small text files placed on your device), including essential, analytical, and tracking cookies;
* web analytics services (e.g., Google Analytics where used); and
* server logs and similar technical records.

Lawful Basis for Processing: This information is processed on the basis of our legitimate interest in ensuring the functionality, performance, security, and optimisation of our website, and-where required-based on user consent to non-essential cookies, in accordance with applicable law.

Control Options: You may manage or disable cookies and similar technologies through your browser settings or consent management tools. Disabling certain cookies may affect the functionality of the website.
Key Clarifications
* Scope of Personal Data: Personal Data does not include information that has been irreversibly anonymised such that you cannot be identified directly or indirectly by us or any other party.

* Business Information: Business Information includes data relating to legal entities (companies, organisations) and their operations where this data can be associated with an identifiable natural person. Where Business Information does not contain Personal Data, data protection laws may still apply to the extent required by applicable legislation.

* No Collection of Sensitive Categories Unless Explicitly Stated: We do not collect special categories of personal data (such as health data, biometric data, racial or ethnic origin) except where you explicitly choose to provide such data for a lawful purpose and we have a lawful basis for processing.
Legal Bases for Processing Personal Data
Under applicable data protection laws, Gragabo processes Personal Data only where we have a valid legal basis. The primary legal bases we rely on are set out below.
1) Consent
Where required by law, we obtain your freely given, specific, informed, and unambiguous consent before processing your Personal Data.
* GDPR: Article 4(11); Article 6(1)(a)
* Serbian Law Reference: Article 12 and Article 4(12) of the Law on Personal Data Protection
Application includes contact forms, diagnostic forms, consultation requests, and marketing communications where consent is specifically required.
Withdrawal: You may withdraw consent at any time. Withdrawal does not affect the lawfulness of processing based on consent prior to withdrawal.
2) Performance of a Contract or Pre-Contractual Steps
Processing is lawful where necessary to perform a contract or to take steps at your request prior to entering into a contract.
* GDPR: Article 6(1)(b)
* Serbian Law Reference: Article 12 of the Law on Personal Data Protection
Application includes providing advisory services, conducting diagnostic assessments, client onboarding, service delivery, invoicing, and support.
3) Legitimate Interests
We process certain Personal Data where it is necessary for our legitimate interests, provided such interests are not overridden by your rights or freedoms.
* GDPR: Article 6(1)(f)
* Serbian Law Reference: Article 12 of the Law on Personal Data Protection
Application includes website analytics, fraud detection, IT security, service improvement, internal business administration, and enforcing our terms.
Balancing: We conduct assessments to ensure legitimate interests do not unduly impact your rights.
4) Compliance with Legal or Regulatory Obligations
We process data to comply with legal duties imposed by statutory authorities.
* GDPR: Article 6(1)(c)
* Serbian Law Reference: Article 12 of the Law on Personal Data Protection
Application includes legal reporting obligations, responding to lawful requests from regulators or courts, and financial record-keeping.
Definitions of Legal Bases

* Consent means a clear and affirmative act signifying your agreement to the processing of your Personal Data for one or more specific purposes. Consent must be freely given, specific, informed, and unambiguous.
* Contractual Necessity means processing necessary to perform a contract to which you are a party, or to take steps you request before entering into such a contract.

* Legitimate Interests means our business interests in operating, analysing, improving, and securing our services and website, provided that such interests are not overridden by your rights, freedoms, or interests.

title: 2. Purpose of Data Processing
intro: Gragabo collects and processes Personal Data and Business Information solely for specified, explicit, legitimate, and lawful purposes, in accordance with Article 5(1)(b) of the GDPR and Article 9 of the Serbian Law on Personal Data Protection. We do not process personal data in a manner that is incompatible with these purposes, unless otherwise permitted or required by applicable law.

subsections.title: 2.1 Primary Purposes of Processing
content: We process Personal Data and Business Information for the following principal purposes:
(a) Communication and Relationship Management To respond to inquiries, communicate regarding services and engagements, and manage professional relationships.
(b) Assessment, Diagnostics, and Evaluation To review diagnostic forms, evaluate operational/financial/strategic information, and prepare professional recommendations.

(c) Service Delivery and Contractual Performance To conduct consultations and assessments, provide advisory and professional services, administer agreements, and manage billing and documentation where applicable.
(d) Website Operation and Technical Optimisation To ensure functionality, security, and stability; analyse performance; improve usability; and prevent misuse and technical faults.

(e) Analytics, Research, and Service Improvement To monitor traffic, conduct statistical analysis, measure effectiveness, and improve services and methodologies. Where reasonably practicable, analytics are conducted using aggregated or anonymised data.
(f) Legal, Regulatory, and Compliance Obligations To comply with legal and professional obligations, respond to lawful requests, maintain required records, and fulfil audit duties.
(g) Protection of Legal and Commercial Interests To establish, exercise, and defend legal claims; protect contractual rights; prevent fraud and abuse; and enforce terms and policies.

subsections.title: 2.2 Secondary and Compatible Processing
content: Where permitted by law, we may further process Personal Data for purposes that are compatible with the original purpose of collection, including internal administration, business continuity planning, professional risk management, and quality assurance.

subsections.title: 2.3 No Commercial Sale or Exploitation of Personal Data
content: Gragabo does not sell, rent, lease, trade, license, monetise, or commercially exploit Personal Data or Business Information. We do not operate a data brokerage model and do not disclose personal data to third parties for independent marketing or resale purposes.

subsections.title: 2.4 Purpose Limitation Safeguard
content: Personal Data shall not be used for purposes that are materially different from those stated above unless required by law, you have been informed in advance, and—where required—your explicit consent has been obtained.

subsections.title: 2.5 Accountability Commitment
content: We maintain internal procedures and documentation to demonstrate compliance with purpose limitation obligations, including access controls, confidentiality duties, and compliance reviews.

title: 3. Data Sharing and Disclosure to Third Parties
intro: Gragabo processes Personal Data primarily within its own organisational structure. We disclose Personal Data to third parties only where such disclosure is lawful, necessary, proportionate, and consistent with the purposes set out in this Policy.

subsections.title: 3.1 Categories of Recipients
content: We may disclose Personal Data and Business Information to the following categories of recipients, strictly on a need-to-know basis:
* Internal Personnel subject to confidentiality, access controls, and relevant obligations.
* Professional Advisors (legal, accounting, audit, compliance, IT/cybersecurity) are bound by confidentiality.
* Service Providers / Data Processors (hosting, communications, video conferencing, CRM, analytics, security) under appropriate contractual safeguards.
* Technology and Infrastructure Partners acting as processors or independent controllers, as legally determined.
* Public Authorities and Legal Bodies where required by law or necessary to protect legal rights.

subsections.title: 3.2 Controller and Processor Roles
content: Depending on the processing activity, Gragabo may act as a data controller or a data processor. Where we act as a processor, we process data strictly in accordance with documented controller instructions and applicable law.

subsections.title: 3.3 Contractual Safeguards and Due Diligence
content: We conduct reasonable due diligence and implement contractual safeguards, including data processing agreements, confidentiality obligations, security requirements, incident response duties, and deletion/return obligations where appropriate.

subsections.title: 3.4 Restrictions on Independent Use
content: Recipients are prohibited from using Personal Data for independent purposes, selling or monetising the data, or combining it with unrelated datasets without lawful basis.

subsections.title: 3.5 Corporate or Structural Transactions
content: In the event of merger, acquisition, restructuring, asset sale, or similar transaction, Personal Data may be disclosed to counterparties and advisers subject to confidentiality and continued compliance obligations.

subsections.title: 3.6 Cross-Border Disclosures
content: Where disclosure involves transfers outside Serbia or the EU/EEA, such transfers are governed by Section 5 and are subject to lawful transfer mechanisms and safeguards.

subsections.title: 3.7 Disclosure with Consent
content: Where disclosure is not otherwise permitted by law, we will seek explicit consent prior to sharing Personal Data.

subsections.title: 3.8 Record-Keeping and Accountability
content: We maintain internal records of material data-sharing activities where required by applicable law.

subsections.title: 3.9 Liability Limitation in Relation to Third Parties
content: While we require third parties to comply with applicable obligations, Gragabo shall not be responsible for unlawful processing by third parties acting outside our documented instructions or contractual scope, except where liability is imposed by mandatory law.

title: 4. Data Security and Personal Data Breach Notification
intro: Gragabo implements and maintains appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risks associated with the processing of Personal Data, in accordance with Article 32 of the GDPR and Article 50 of the Serbian Law on Personal Data Protection.

subsections.title: 4.1 Technical and Organisational Security Measures
content: We rely on a combination of internal controls and secure third-party infrastructure. Measures may include:
* reputable providers for hosting, domain management, email, and cloud infrastructure;
* secure servers, firewalls, and access controls maintained by service providers;
* encrypted connections (HTTPS/SSL) for website access and transmission;
* role-based access controls and least-privilege access;
* internal confidentiality obligations and secure-handling policies.

subsections.title: 4.2 Third-Party Security Controls
content: Where third parties process Personal Data on our behalf, they are engaged under safeguards designed to ensure confidentiality, integrity, availability, and resilience.

subsections.title: 4.3 No Absolute Security Guarantee
content: While we take reasonable and proportionate measures, we cannot guarantee absolute security or eliminate all cyber risks.

subsections.title: 4.4 Personal Data Breach Response and Notification
content: A “personal data breach” means a breach of security leading to accidental or unlawful destruction, loss, alteration, unauthorised disclosure of, or access to Personal Data.
4.4.1 Incident Detection and Response

Upon becoming aware of a suspected or confirmed breach, we follow a response procedure designed to contain and mitigate, secure systems, assess scope and impact, implement corrective measures, and document actions.
4.4.2 Notification to Supervisory Authorities (72-Hour Rule)

Where a breach is likely to result in a risk to individuals’ rights and freedoms, we will notify the competent supervisory authority without undue delay and, where feasible, not later than 72 hours after becoming aware of the breach.
Where Gragabo acts as a processor, it will notify the relevant controller without undue delay.
4.4.3 Communication to Affected Individuals

Where a breach is likely to result in a high risk to individuals, we will communicate the breach to affected data subjects without undue delay, in clear and plain language, including appropriate mitigation guidance.
4.4.4 Allocation of Responsibility and Liability

Gragabo remains responsible for safeguards and notification obligations where it acts as controller. Where third-party providers process data outside documented instructions or contractual scope, they may bear independent responsibility under applicable law. Nothing in this Policy limits or excludes liability that cannot be excluded under mandatory law.

subsections.title: 4.5 Continuous Improvement
content: We periodically review and update security measures to reflect evolving risks, legal requirements, and operational growth.

title: 5. International Data Transfers
intro: Gragabo operates in an international business environment and maintains professional relationships across multiple jurisdictions, including the EU/EEA, CEFTA member states, the United States, Mexico, Russia, India, and regions across Asia, Africa, North America, South America, Europe, and Australia.
Personal Data and Business Information may, in certain circumstances, be transferred to, stored in, or accessed from jurisdictions outside the Republic of Serbia and outside the EU/EEA.
No transfer is carried out unless a lawful transfer mechanism and appropriate safeguards are in place.

subsections.title: 5.1 General Principles
content: Transfers are limited to what is necessary, assessed case-by-case, and implemented with safeguards to ensure effective protection and enforceable rights.

subsections.title: 5.2 Adequacy Decisions
content: Where an adequacy decision applies, transfers may occur on that basis, in accordance with applicable law.

subsections.title: 5.3 Appropriate Safeguards
content: Where no adequacy decision exists, transfers may rely on appropriate safeguards, including Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) and supplementary measures where required.

subsections.title: 5.4 Transfers to Service Providers and Infrastructure Partners
content: Personal Data may be processed through third-party providers (cloud, email, hosting, collaboration). We verify appropriate safeguards and contractual protections prior to engagement.

subsections.title: 5.5 Derogations for Specific Situations
content: In limited circumstances and only where permitted, transfers may rely on specific derogations (e.g., explicit consent; contractual necessity; legal claims; vital interests), applied restrictively.

subsections.title: 5.6 Transfers Within Global Business Operations
content: International cooperation is governed by confidentiality obligations, data protection clauses, and applicable transfer safeguards.

subsections.title: 5.7 Ongoing Assessment
content: Where required, we assess relevant risks, enforceability, and legal remedies, and implement supplementary safeguards or restrict transfers accordingly.

subsections.title: 5.8 Rights in Relation to Transfers
content: Individuals may request information about transfer mechanisms and safeguards and may lodge complaints with competent authorities.

subsections.title: 5.9 Limitation of Unlawful Transfers
content: Unauthorised cross-border transfers are prohibited and may result in corrective action and legal measures.

subsections.title: 5.10 Regulatory Developments
content: We monitor legal developments and update transfer mechanisms and safeguards where required.

title: 6. Data Retention, Archiving, and Secure Disposal
intro: Gragabo retains Personal Data only for as long as is objectively necessary to fulfil lawful purposes and obligations. No Personal Data is retained indefinitely.

Retention decisions are based on documented criteria, including purpose, sensitivity, contractual duration, limitation periods, legal record-keeping obligations, audit needs, and dispute-resolution requirements. Data may be archived with restricted access where legally required. When no longer required, data is securely deleted, destroyed, or anonymised, as appropriate. Legal holds may suspend deletion where litigation, audits, investigations, or disputes are pending or reasonably anticipated.

title: 7. Data Subject Rights, Exercise Procedures, and Enforcement Mechanisms
intro: Gragabo recognises and respects the rights of individuals whose Personal Data it processes, including rights of access, rectification, erasure, restriction, portability, and objection, and rights relating to automated decision-making where applicable.

Requests may be submitted using the contact details in Section 10. We may verify identity to prevent unauthorised disclosure. Requests are handled without undue delay and generally within one month, subject to lawful extensions for complex matters. Requests are ordinarily free of charge; however, manifestly unfounded or excessive requests may be subject to a reasonable fee or refusal, as permitted by law.
Data subjects may lodge complaints with competent supervisory authorities and seek judicial remedies, as set out in Section 10.

title: 8. Complaints Handling, Regulatory Cooperation, and Dispute Resolution
intro: Gragabo maintains internal procedures to receive, assess, investigate, and respond to privacy complaints fairly and promptly. Where a complaint is substantiated, we implement proportionate corrective measures. We cooperate in good faith with competent supervisory authorities and comply with lawful requests and binding decisions. Disputes may be addressed through amicable resolution or alternative dispute mechanisms where appropriate, without prejudice to statutory rights and judicial remedies.

title: 9. Policy Updates, Amendments, Version Control, and Governance Oversight
intro: This Privacy Policy is reviewed and updated where necessary to reflect legal changes, regulatory guidance, judicial decisions, technological developments, and operational growth. Amendments are subject to internal governance and approval. Material changes may be notified via website publication and, where appropriate, direct communication. Archived versions are retained for compliance, audit, and evidentiary purposes. No amendment retroactively limits statutory rights or circumvents mandatory obligations.

title: 10. Contact Information, Supervisory Authority, Jurisdiction, and Governing Law

subsections.title: 10.1 Identity of the Data Controller
content: Gragabo DOO Beograd Registered in the Republic of Serbia Registered seat: Obilićev venac 18–20, Belgrade 11000, Serbia Company registration number: 115466511 Email: international@gragabo.com Website: www.gragabo.com

Gragabo determines the purposes and means of processing Personal Data in connection with its website and advisory services, except where it acts as a processor under separate contractual arrangements.

subsections.title: 10.2 Data Protection Contact Point
content: All inquiries relating to Personal Data processing, rights requests, international transfers, complaints, or this Privacy Policy may be directed to: international@gragabo.com. We may request additional information where necessary to verify identity and prevent unauthorised disclosure.

subsections.title: 10.3 Competent Supervisory Authority
content: Republic of Serbia: Commissioner for Information of Public Importance and Personal Data Protection Bulevar kralja Aleksandra 15 11000 Belgrade, Republic of Serbia
EU/EEA (where GDPR applies): Data subjects may lodge complaints with the supervisory authority of their habitual residence, place of work, or the place of the alleged infringement.

subsections.title: 10.4 Governing Law
content: This Privacy Policy is governed by the laws of the Republic of Serbia, including the Law on Personal Data Protection, and-where applicable-the GDPR and other mandatory data protection regulations that apply by territorial scope. Where multiple regimes apply, this Policy is interpreted to ensure compliance with the highest applicable standard.

subsections.title: 10.5 Jurisdiction
content: Without prejudice to statutory rights of data subjects, disputes relating to this Privacy Policy are subject to the jurisdiction of the competent courts of the Republic of Serbia, unless mandatory provisions of EU or other applicable law require otherwise. Nothing limits the right to initiate proceedings before courts or supervisory authorities where permitted by law.

subsections.title: 10.6 International Regulatory Interaction
content: Where Gragabo processes Personal Data of individuals located outside Serbia, including within the EU/EEA, Gragabo cooperates with competent authorities as required by applicable law.

subsections.title: 10.7 Severability
content: If any provision of this Policy is found invalid or unenforceable, it shall be severed to the extent necessary and the remainder shall remain in full force and effect.

subsections.title: 10.8 Entire Privacy Framework
content: This Privacy Policy constitutes the complete statement of Gragabo’s data protection practices in relation to its website and services, subject to specific contractual arrangements, additional notices at the point of collection, and mandatory statutory requirements. In the event of conflict, mandatory law prevails.
